project: Sarcasm Detection from Social Media Text using the SARC Dataset
objective: Design a fair, reproducible comparison between BERTweet and RoBERTa under two preprocessing settings: stopwords kept and selective stopword removal.

input_strategy.description: Use only textual content. No metadata is used. The parent_comment is treated as textual context and comment is treated as the target reply. During tokenization, these should be passed as a text pair where possible.

versionA.name: Version A - stopwords kept
versionA.description: Preprocessing Version A keeps stopwords, punctuation, casing, and ALL CAPS to preserve sarcasm signals.

versionB.name: Version B - selective stopword removal
versionB.description: Preprocessing Version B removes common stopwords while preserving negation words such as not, no, never, don't, doesn't, didn't.

split_design.rule: Use exactly the same split indices for Version A and Version B so that stopword impact is compared fairly.

tokenization_design.rule: Use each model's official Hugging Face tokenizer. Do not manually remove punctuation during tokenization.

training_design.note: These are default experimental settings. They can be tuned later if hardware limits require smaller batch size.

model_selection_rule.final_demo_rule: Train and compare all four experiments, but deploy only the best-performing model in the final interface.

experiments.purpose: Evaluate BERTweet when stopwords and sarcasm-related surface signals are preserved.
Evaluate whether selective stopword removal improves or hurts BERTweet performance.
Evaluate RoBERTa when stopwords and sarcasm-related surface signals are preserved.
Evaluate whether selective stopword removal improves or hurts RoBERTa performance.

academic_rationale.why_two_models: BERTweet is specialized for social media text, while RoBERTa is a strong general transformer baseline. Comparing them helps determine whether social-media pretraining improves sarcasm detection on SARC.
academic_rationale.why_two_preprocessing_versions: The lecturer requested checking whether removing stopwords helps or hurts. Sarcasm may depend on small words and sentence structure, so the effect must be tested rather than assumed.
academic_rationale.why_one_final_model: The final application should be simple and reproducible. After fair comparison, only the best-performing model will be selected for the final demo.
